articles.title: Condom maker relocates headquarters to historic Lynn dairy creamery
articles.description:
Global Protection Corp says its move makes Lynn the nation’s condom capital, with more condoms shipped in and out of the city than anywhere else in the nation.

The post Condom maker relocates headquarters to historic Lynn dairy creamery appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Global Protection Corp relocates headquarters to historic dairy creamery in Lynn.
Lynn becomes the nation's condom capital, surpassing all other cities in shipments.
Local economy may benefit from increased jobs and business activity in Lynn.

articles.description:
"This is for all the people of Paraguay."

summary: Paraguay's victory over Germany marked a historic upset in World Cup history.

Local businesses reported increased engagement and support from fans after the match.

Rising fear among immigrant communities reported following the June 30, 2026 decision.

articles.title: In San Francisco, even $180,000 tech salaries are no longer enough
articles.description:
As OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to go public, tech workers making six figures are grousing that they cannot compete with the new AI elite. Some doubt they can afford to stay.

The post In San Francisco, even $180,000 tech salaries are no longer enough appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tech workers in San Francisco struggle with high living costs despite $180,000 salaries.
OpenAI and Anthropic's public offerings intensify competition for tech talent and salaries.
Boston tech professionals may face similar pressures as AI companies grow and attract talent.

articles.title: ‘Nobody’s going first’: Cannabis cafes bleeding cash waiting to open, six months after regulations passed
articles.description:
Amsterdam-style cannabis lounges are still coming to Massachusetts — just much slower than many business owners expected. It’s been six months since the Cannabis Control Commission approved regulations for these businesses. Yet almost no towns have passed zoning changes for the ventures to move forward — and the commission has not opened the licenses up […]

The post ‘Nobody’s going first’: Cannabis cafes bleeding cash waiting to open, six months after regulations passed appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Cannabis cafes in Massachusetts await zoning changes to open, six months post-regulation approval.
Only a few towns have initiated zoning changes for cannabis lounges since June 2023.
Local business owners face financial strain as they wait for licenses and operational approval.

articles.title: Inspired by its anti-Belichick coach, Paraguay forged a famous win in Foxborough
articles.description:
After decades of hosting high-profile international soccer games, the region finally got to see the agony of penalty kicks, and the joy of an upset victory.

The post Inspired by its anti-Belichick coach, Paraguay forged a famous win in Foxborough appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Paraguay defeated Germany in a penalty shootout during a World Cup match in Foxborough.
This marked the first major upset in Foxborough's history of hosting international soccer games.
Local fans experienced a mix of joy and agony, enhancing Boston's sports culture.

articles.title: Jaylen Brown has reportedly ‘grown frustrated’ with Celtics amid offseason rife with trade rumors

Boston fans may face uncertainty regarding Brown's future with the team as the season approaches.

articles.title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras sheds tears, gets ejected in emotional night at Fenway Park
articles.description:
